prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model for stock prices. As an AI, I do not have real-time market data access, so I've used estimated parameters. For NEE, a utility stock, we assume an annual drift (expected return) of 8% and an annual volatility of 20%, which are typical values for stocks in this sector. The time horizon of 21 calendar days is approximated as 15 trading days for calculations. The point estimate represents the expected price under this model, and the confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution of prices based on the assumed drift, volatility, and time horizon.
